changes_summary: Sentence-level revision addressing the four new v3 flags without expanding scope: (1) issue_hints + all_locations (same hand-off concern): §9 now names the full migration chain — author in `.migrations_staging/`, copy verbatim into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md`, apply via `supabase db push`, verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`; §10 uses the same chain for `DROP TABLE`. (2) correctness-1 (vacuous dev arm in cross-window tally): dropped the `topics GROUP BY environment` SQL; §6 now has a prod-only `topics` query plus an explicit instruction to source the Phase-1 shadow partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), citing the Phase-1 publishing-OFF lines (:611, :627, :633) so the operator does not misread \"0 dev rows\" as \"dev clean.\" §2/§3/§4 reinforce the annotation. (3) correctness-2 (thin Branch B SQL verification): §5 Branch B now names the Discord-side count (scroll the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal and demotes any SQL suppression marker to nice-to-have; added a fifth TODO(phase-1) for the new-editor / legacy bot identity distinction. (4) scope (binary A/B framing overconfident): §1 provisional banner now contains an explicit \"if Phase 1 ships any third mechanism (hybrid, per-guild config, per-channel rollout) → ABORT to consult-the-doc escape hatch\" clause; §3 preconditions adds \"Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)\" as a gate; §7 decision tree adds a corresponding row. Length budget preserved — all changes are sentence-level edits to existing sections. SD-1..SD-7 settled decisions and doc-only scope preserved.

flags_addressed.reason: §9 step 1 now names the full hand-off chain explicitly: author in .migrations_staging/ → copy VERBATIM (filename unchanged) into /Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/ per .migrations_staging/README.md → apply via `supabase db push` → verify the row recorded in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations before treating as applied. §10 step 2 uses the same chain for the DROP TABLE block. Operator cannot write SQL into staging and assume it ran.

Replaced the v2 `topics GROUP BY environment` cross-window tally (which would return 0 dev rows by design and mislead the operator) with a prod-only SQL count plus an explicit instruction in §6 to add the Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per :611-644), citing :611/:627/:633 (Phase 1 publishing OFF) for why dev `topics` has no `partial` rows. Trip-wire is now sum-of-two-sources. The Fast Triage `-- swap to 'dev'` annotation in §2/§3/§4 also notes the dev rows are diagnostic only.

§5 Branch B verification now names the Discord-side count (operator scrolls the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal. The SQL suppression marker is demoted to a nice-to-have secondary check that is acceptable to skip if Phase 1 doesn't write one. A fifth TODO(phase-1) is added to the §1 banner for the new-editor / legacy bot identity distinction so the Discord-side count has a concrete anchor.

Same fix as issue_hints — §9 names the full chain (`.migrations_staging/` author → `supabase/migrations/` apply destination → `supabase db push` → verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`). §10 uses the same chain. The destination path is given as an absolute path per `.migrations_staging/README.md`.

§1 provisional banner now includes an explicit out-of-scope clause: 'if Phase 1 ships any mechanism other than Branch A or Branch B (hybrid, per-guild config branch, per-channel rollout, partial guild rollout), ABORT to the consult-the-doc escape hatch and do not improvise.' §3 preconditions adds 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' as a gate. §7 decision tree adds a row: 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism → ABORT immediately; the runbook does not cover it.'

Already addressed in v2 (verification anchors on next observed `live_update_editor_runs` row with `status='completed'`, not a fixed window). v3 self-review pass re-verifies this in Step 13.9; no further change needed.
Already addressed in v2 via Branch A / Branch B branching. v3 reinforces by demoting SQL marker to nice-to-have under Branch B and naming Discord-side count as primary; no scope expansion.

Already addressed in v2 (lease symptoms split into three distinct rows: new-editor self-contention HOT-FIX, legacy writing to new tables ABORT, lease-key overlap ABORT). v3 self-review pass re-verifies in Step 13.11; no further change needed.

Already addressed in v2 via the provisional banner. v3 strengthens by listing five TODO(phase-1) items (adding bot identity), explicit third-mechanism abort clause, and the corresponding precondition gate and decision-tree row.

questions: Phase 1 has not landed yet — what is the chosen name and location of the publishing toggle (env var, config row, feature flag)?
Does Phase 1 suppress the legacy editor at the scheduler-tick level (Branch A) or at the publish-call level (Branch B)? Or some third mechanism — in which case the runbook directs ABORT.
If Branch B, does Phase 1 write a suppression marker (column or metadata key) on `live_update_editor_runs`? If not, Discord-side count is the only signal — acceptable but thin.
What is the new-editor bot identity in the live Discord channel, and is it observably distinct from the legacy editor's bot identity? Affects the Branch-B Discord-side count signal.
Which Discord channel is the prod equivalent of `#main_test` for the trace embed during the 24-48h watch?
Does Phase 1 use `supabase db push` to apply migrations against prod, or a different applier (Supabase dashboard SQL editor, psql-direct)? Affects §9 step 1 apply mechanism.

success_criteria.criterion: File `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md` exists at exactly that path with non-empty content.
Document contains a Fast Triage SQL section querying `topics` grouped by `publication_status` scoped to `environment='prod'`, matching the `docs/runbook-payments.md` Fast Triage pattern.

Document includes a 'Provisional until Phase 1 lands' banner in §1 that enumerates five TODO(phase-1) items (toggle name + location, suppression mechanism, Branch-B run-row suppression marker, new-editor / legacy bot identity, prod trace-embed channel) AND contains an explicit clause: if Phase 1 ships any mechanism other than Branch A or Branch B (hybrid, per-guild config, per-channel rollout), ABORT to the consult-the-doc escape hatch.

Document's pre-flip preconditions checklist includes 'Phase 1 shipped Branch A or Branch B (not a third mechanism)' as a gate alongside the other gates (Phase 1 soak, schema migration applied, indexes present, backfill parity, lease in place, trace embed wired, legacy editor still running, paged-in window, Phase-1 TODOs resolved).

Document includes a GO / TUNE / ABORT shadow-mode-to-cutover comparison rubric keyed on at least: replay-divergence explainability, collision-override rate, `record_observation` rate, Phase-1 shadow-attempted-publish partials sourced from Phase-1 telemetry (NOT from `topics WHERE environment='dev'`), schema-rejection rate, cost/latency.

Document includes a Publishing-flag-flip procedure with ordered steps, a `<LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE>` placeholder, branch-A/branch-B verification shapes, and explicit anchoring on the next observed `live_update_editor_runs` row (not a fixed 15-minute window).

Document's Branch B flip-verification names the Discord-side count (scroll the live channel since the flip timestamp for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity) as the PRIMARY signal, with any SQL suppression-marker check demoted to a nice-to-have secondary that may not exist if Phase 1 ships no marker.
Document's flip-verification does NOT check for 'zero new rows in legacy live_update_feed_items' (legacy table writes continue during the watch per `:651`).

Document includes four partial-publish detection SQL queries (windowed counts, per-row deep-dive, still-pending-too-long, rejection-rate) scoped to `environment='prod'` with `-- swap to 'dev'` annotations, plus a prod-only `topics` trip-wire count AND an explicit instruction to source the Phase-1 shadow partial count from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per `:611-644`), citing the Phase-1 publishing-OFF lines `:611`/`:627`/`:633` for why `topics WHERE environment='dev'` is vacuous.

Document includes a 24-48h watch decision-tree table (SYMPTOM | THRESHOLD | ACTION | POINTER) that maps the partial-publish trip-wire to '(Phase-1 telemetry shadow partials) + (Phase-2 prod partials from topics) cumulative ≥ 2 → ABORT + restore topic_publications' citing `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:600-601`.

Document's decision tree splits lease symptoms into three distinct rows with distinct actions (new-editor self-contention HOT-FIX, legacy writing to new tables ABORT, new+legacy run-row overlap on same lease key ABORT) AND includes a row mapping 'Phase 1 turns out to be a third mechanism' → ABORT.

Document includes a Rollback procedure (runner-pointer flip) that explicitly leaves `topics` rows untouched, distinguishes pre-rename rollback (trivial — toggle flip) from post-rename rollback (reverse rename SQL), cites `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:596`, and covers both Branch-A and Branch-B verification on legacy-publish resumption.

Document includes an ordered table-rename migration SQL block that renames all six legacy tables (`live_update_feed_items`, `live_update_watchlist`, `live_update_duplicate_state`, `live_update_editorial_memory`, `live_update_candidates`, `live_update_decisions`) to `_legacy_<name>`, retains `live_update_editor_runs`, runs inside `BEGIN; ... COMMIT;` with a dry-run rollback note, includes row-count parity SELECT before AND after, and a constraint-preservation note for Postgres.

Document names the full migration hand-off chain: author in `.migrations_staging/` (timestamped filename) → copy verbatim into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md` → apply via `supabase db push` (or Phase-1 alternate) → verify recorded in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`.

Document's §9 cross-checks the six-table set against `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223` and `src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968` — NOT against `docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md`'s 'Legacy Storage And Helpers' section.
Document explicitly states that the table rename runs only AFTER the 24-48h watch passes, so rollback during the watch remains a single toggle flip.

Document includes a deletion-schedule section that records the `_legacy_*` deletion 2-4 weeks post-rename as an operator-filled blank (no hardcoded date), plus a final `DROP TABLE _legacy_*` block authored via the same `.migrations_staging/` → `supabase/migrations/` → `supabase db push` hand-off, and named bring-forward / push-out circumstances.
Document references `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md` for schema shape rather than re-listing column definitions, and points back to it as the ABORT-to-rollback escape hatch in a footer.

Document references `src/features/summarising/live_update_editor.py` (specifically `_publish_accepted_candidates` / `_select_publishable_candidates`) as the legacy fallback path the runner-pointer rollback re-enables.
Document length is roughly comparable to `docs/runbook-payments.md` (~400 lines, give or take 100); not under 200 and not over 700.
Tone matches `docs/runbook-payments.md` — terse, imperative, copy-paste SQL ready, minimal narrative prose.
Every `TODO(phase-1)` placeholder in the doc is clearly labeled so the operator can spot Phase-1-dependent gaps; total of five TODOs as listed in §1.
No code edits outside `docs/live-update-editor-phase-2-runbook.md`; existing tests still pass (unchanged).

assumptions: The publish toggle exists at a single named location chosen by Phase 1; the runbook references it as `<LIVE_EDITOR_PUBLISH_TOGGLE>` with a `TODO(phase-1)` marker.

Phase 1 ships exactly one of Branch A (scheduler-tick gate) or Branch B (publish-call gate at `_publish_accepted_candidates` / `_select_publishable_candidates`). If Phase 1 ships any third mechanism (hybrid, per-guild config branch, per-channel rollout, partial guild rollout), the runbook directs the operator to ABORT before flipping; the runbook does not cover those shapes.

Branch B's primary verification signal is a Discord-side count (operator scrolls the live channel for posts attributed to the legacy bot identity since the flip timestamp). A SQL suppression marker on `live_update_editor_runs` is a nice-to-have secondary check that may not exist (early-return at `_publish_accepted_candidates` need not record anything).

The new editor's bot identity in the live Discord channel is distinct from the legacy editor's bot identity (or otherwise observably labeled). If they share an identity, the Discord-side count is not a reliable signal — this is captured as a fifth TODO(phase-1) and would force ABORT.

Per `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:611`, `:627`, `:633`, Phase 1 runs with publishing OFF, so `topics WHERE environment='dev' AND publication_status='partial'` cannot exist by design. The cross-window partial-publish trip-wire therefore sources Phase-1 shadow partials from Phase-1 shadow-mode telemetry (trace embed + shadow logs per `:611-644`), NOT from `topics`.
Per `:651`, during the 24-48h watch both editors continue to write to their own tables; only Discord-send is suppressed. §5 verification targets Discord-send activity.
Cadence is hourly per `:633`; verification anchors on the next observed `live_update_editor_runs` row with `status='completed'` after the flip timestamp.

Postgres is the target DB and `ALTER TABLE ... RENAME TO` preserves indexes, sequences, FK constraints, and RLS. The migration hand-off chain is: author in `.migrations_staging/`, copy verbatim into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` per `.migrations_staging/README.md`, apply via `supabase db push`, verify in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`. If Phase 1 chose a different applier (Supabase dashboard SQL editor, psql-direct), a TODO(phase-1) covers the swap.

The six legacy tables to rename are the set in `docs/live-update-editor-redesign.md:212-223`, confirmed against `src/common/storage_handler.py:652-1968`. `docs/live-update-editor-legacy-boundaries.md`'s 'Legacy Storage And Helpers' section is about `daily_summaries` and is NOT the cross-reference; `live_update_editor_runs` is retained.
Deletion date is operator-chosen between 2 and 4 weeks post-rename per `:653`; runbook leaves a blank line.
ABORT is the default response to any unlisted symptom or any Phase-1 mechanism other than Branch A/B.
Doc is the only artifact this plan produces — no code, no tests.
